axis: interp dispatch arm file_size — stat-based, multi-GB + binary safe
trigger: ω-stdlib-2 closure noted hexa_full.hexa:10606 dispatch arm bypassed the rt_file_size builtin fix; ω-audio-3 confirmed binary NULL truncation when calling file_size on WAV files

bugs: multi-GB read into RAM (vs O(1) stat)
binary NULL truncate (read_file returns string; len uses C-string strlen which stops at first NUL)

after.mirrors: rt_file_size in runtime.c:3707 + pfs_file_size in stdlib/portable_fs.hexa

file_exists_dispatch_arms_audited.verdict: BOTH ALREADY CORRECT — they delegate to the rt_file_exists C builtin (`file_exists(args[0].str_val)` is a host call), which ω-stdlib-2 already fixed. No additional dispatch-arm changes needed.

scope_note: hexa_full.hexa interp dispatch chain has many built-in arms; this cycle fixed only the file_size arm because (a) it was the documented bug from stdlib-2, (b) ω-audio-3 hit it on binary WAV reads, (c) other arms either delegate to C builtins (file_exists) or have unrelated semantics (clock, chr, etc.).
closure_status: COMPLETE — single dispatch arm, two-line semantic fix, validated PASS, host interp rebuilt and current
rollback: git revert <commit-hash>; restore prior 8-line dispatch arm with read_file+strlen pattern
